rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY SINGER VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 19, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER ANTHONY SINGER's Hearing For Writ Of Mandate. By a June 7, 2019 order, petitioner Anthony Singer was awarded his court costs in this California Public Records Act matter under Government Code §6259(d). The matter was continued to establish the amount of costs. Singer filed a June 12 declaration claiming $990. On June 21, respondent San Francisco Police Department filed a non-opposition to that amount of costs. Petitioner Singer is thus hereby awarded $990 in costs to be paid by respondent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA NENAYDOKH VS. GREG SHENKMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtor Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court For Violating Discovery Orders.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2019, Line 1, To Show Cause Why Judgment Debtor Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court For Violating Discovery Orders. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. It appears from the docket that moving party and judgment creditor has not yet been able to comply with Judge Schulman's June 19 order, which required, inter alia, proof of personal service on Greg Shenkman. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT J. MANDELL ET AL VS. GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Scott J. Mandell. Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 7-3-19.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2019, Line 2, Defendant's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Scott J. Mandell. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition. Plaintiff Scott J. Mandell shall appear for deposition within 10 days of service of this order at a mutually agreeable time at 400 Oyster Point Blvd. Sanctions in the amount of $1,000 are imposed against plaintiff Mandell and his attorney Robert Knox, jointly and severally, to be paid to plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant C.I. Energia Solar S.A.S. - E.S. Windows To Attend Deposition And Produce Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against C.I. Energia Solar S.A.S. - E.S. Windows
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, July 19, 2019, Line 3, CROSS-DEFENDANT SWINERTON BUILDERS' Motion To Compel Cross-Complainant C.I. Energia Solar S.A.S. - E.S. Windows To Attend Deposition And Produce Documents; And Request For Sanctions Against C.I. Energia Solar S.A.S. - E.S. Windows. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Moving party Swinerton Builders wishes to depose the designated corporate representative of C.I. Energia Solar SA, a company that has apparently not designated a principal place of business in California, but chose to initiate litigation in this forum. Under 2025.250(d), "If an organization has not designated a principal executive or business office in California, the deposition shall be taken at a place that is, at the option of the party giving notice of the deposition, either within the county where the action is pending, or within 75 miles of any executive or business office in California of the organization." The deposition shall occur in San Francisco within 20 days of service of this order. The Court declines to award sanctions, as both sides acted with substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THIERRY HUGAND ET AL VS. ANDRE E. BENGUEREL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 19, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT ANDRE BENGUEREL's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From The Complaint. CCP §435.5 requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a motion to strike may be filed. Counsel's declaration indicates that no such meet-and-confer has occurred here, so this motion is ordered OFF CALENDAR to allow compliance. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant has until August 19, 2019 to respond to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)
